exercises.title: FizzBuzz Classic
exercises.description: Print numbers 1–20. For multiples of 3 print 'Fizz', for multiples of 5 print 'Buzz', for multiples of both print 'FizzBuzz', otherwise print the number.
exercises.expectedOutput:
1
2
Fizz
4
Buzz
Fizz
7
8
Fizz
Buzz
11
Fizz
13
14
FizzBuzz
16
17
Fizz
19
Buzz
hints: Check the combined condition (divisible by both 3 and 5) first.

exercises.title: Prime Number Checker
exercises.description: Print all prime numbers from 2 to 30. A prime has no divisors other than 1 and itself.
hints: A number is prime if no divisor from 2 to n-1 divides it evenly.
Use break to stop checking once a divisor is found.

exercises.title: Simple ATM Simulation (Pre-set Operations)
exercises.description: Simulate a series of ATM operations on an account. Process deposits, withdrawals (with balance checks), and print a transaction log.
exercises.starterCode:
# Starting balance
balance = 500.00

# Pre-set list of operations: (type, amount)
operations = [
    ("deposit", 200.00),
    ("withdraw", 150.00),
    ("withdraw", 700.00),   # should be rejected
    ("deposit", 50.00),
    ("withdraw", 100.00),
]

print("=== ATM Transaction Log ===")
print(f"Opening balance: ${balance:.2f}")
print("-" * 30)

for op_type, amount in operations:
    if op_type == "deposit":
        balance += amount
        print(f"Deposit   +${amount:.2f} -> Balance: ${balance:.2f}")
    elif op_type == "withdraw":
        if amount > balance:
            print(f"Withdraw  -${amount:.2f} -> REJECTED (insufficient funds)")
        else:
            balance -= amount
            print(f"Withdraw  -${amount:.2f} -> Balance: ${balance:.2f}")

print("-" * 30)
print(f"Closing balance: ${balance:.2f}")
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== ATM Transaction Log ===
Opening balance: $500.00
------------------------------
Deposit   +$200.00 -> Balance: $700.00
Withdraw  -$150.00 -> Balance: $550.00
Withdraw  -$700.00 -> REJECTED (insufficient funds)
Deposit   +$50.00 -> Balance: $600.00
Withdraw  -$100.00 -> Balance: $500.00
------------------------------
Closing balance: $500.00
hints: Use a for loop to iterate over the list of operations.
Check amount > balance before allowing a withdrawal.
Use f-string formatting with :.2f for monetary amounts.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Course 3 Control Flow Summary
warmup.description: All control flow in one block: if/elif/else, for, while, break, continue.
warmup.starterCode:
# if/elif/else
score = 88
if score >= 90: grade = "A"
elif score >= 80: grade = "B"
else: grade = "C"
print(f"Grade: {grade}")

# for loop with continue
for i in range(1, 6):
    if i == 3: continue
    print(i, end=" ")
print()

# while with break
n = 1
while True:
    if n * n > 50:
        break
    n += 1
print(f"First n where n^2 > 50: {n}")
warmup.expectedOutput:
Grade: B
1 2 4 5 
First n where n^2 > 50: 8
